concerns.text: Task T011 (Implementation of generate_partitions.py) explicitly requires loading 'tests/data/reference_values.csv' to assert exact integer matches. However, the producer task for this artifact, T008, is listed in Phase 2 (Foundational) but is marked [P] (parallel-safe) alongside T004-T007. If T011 executes before T008 completes (due to parallel execution of Phase 2), the file will be missing, causing a runtime failure. T008 must be a strict sequential prerequisite for T011, or T011 must be moved to Phase 3 with a hard dependency on T008 completion.

Task T016a (feature_engineering.py) depends on 'data/raw/partitions_raw.csv'. The producer of this artifact is T012 (US1 Implementation). While the text notes US2 depends on US1, T016a is marked [P] (parallel-safe). If T016a runs in parallel with T012 (or before T012 finishes), it will fail to find the input file. The [P] tag is incorrect here; T016a must be strictly sequential after T012.

Task T024 (CV logic in regression_model.py) depends on the fitted model and data from T017a/b/c (US2 Implementation). T024 is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T017a/b/c are listed as US2 Implementation tasks. If T024 runs in parallel with T017a, the model object or results file will not exist. T024 must be strictly sequential after T017c.

Task T013b (Revision Task: regime detection) is marked [P] and placed in Phase 3. It modifies 'code/generate_partitions.py'. However, T011 (the main implementation of generate_partitions.py) is also in Phase 3. If T013b runs in parallel with T011, there is a race condition on the source file. Furthermore, T013b depends on the logic in T011 being present to add the detection. T013b should be sequential after T011, not parallel.

Task T008 description says 'Create tests/data/reference_values.csv... for all n in a finite range'. The specific range (n=100) is only clarified in T011. For strict ordering clarity, T008 should explicitly state 'for n in [1, 100]' to ensure the consumer (T011) knows the exact artifact scope without cross-referencing a later task.
